title: Bring up and tune a control loop safely
description: Stand up a control loop incrementally with limits and stop in place before tuning gains on real hardware.
body: Steps: 1. Implement the loop with actuator limits and an emergency stop active from the first command. 2. Validate timing in simulation: fixed period, bounded jitter, no missed cycles. 3. Tune gains in simulation against a model, checking step response for stability and overshoot. 4. Move to hardware at reduced authority (low speed/torque), verifying limits and stop work. 5. Increase authority gradually while watching for oscillation or saturation. Rollback point: before raising gains or actuator authority. Validation: the loop is stable in sim and on hardware at full authority with bounded jitter, and the stop halts motion at every stage; never tune aggressive gains directly on hardware without limits and a tested stop in place.
